title: 7 Settlement of a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
body_text:
7	Settlement of a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
7.1	Summary of Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account Settlement
Settlement of Cheques Processed in Branch Capture
Central 1 credits your financial institutionâs Central 1 account for the value of cheques processed in Branch Capture. Branch Capture deposits appear on your financial institutionâs statement (AIBC/AIBU for BC, AIOC/AIOU for Ontario) as code âBCâ.
Central 1 may issue a deposit adjustment for cheques processed via Branch Capture if:
an item was deposited that canât be processed (e.g., image quality problems, piggybacks);
an item amount was keyed incorrectly, and a deposit was force balanced; or
an item was received that shouldnât have been included in the deposit (e.g., the currency of the item doesnât match the currency of the deposit type, the item isnât eligible for clearing).
See the Deposit Processing Part for more information on deposit adjustments.
Settlement for Receiver General Concentrator Holding Accounts
When Central 1 initiates the sweep, a Receiver General Concentrator holding account is debited for the deposit total(s) that you report on the Receiver General Deposit Request in ServiceNow. These transactions appear on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report. The holding account balance should zero at the end of each processing cycle.
You must monitor and reconcile the Receiver General Concentrator holding account daily. If thereâs a problem with a pre-authorized debit (PAD), donât return the PAD through the clearing. If an error is identified, submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request form in ServiceNow. See Section 6.2, Managing Deposit Errors for a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account, for procedures.
Central 1 assesses the total value to apply to the Receiver Generalâs Concentrator account held at Central 1. Central 1 sends the Receiver General a wire transfer and a summary of deposits by Routing Authorization Number.
END
